section_name: Registration by Mail or Electronic Means; Service Charge
text:
(a) A county assessor-collector may retain a service charge in the amount determined by the board under Section 502.1911 (Registration Processing and Handling Fee) from each applicant registering a vehicle by mail. The service charge shall be used to pay the costs of handling and postage to mail the registration receipt and insignia to the applicant.

(b) With the approval of the commissioners court of a county, a county assessor-collector may contract with a private entity to enable an applicant for registration to use an electronic off-premises location. A private entity may retain an amount determined by the board under Section 502.1911 (Registration Processing and Handling Fee) for the service provided.

(c) The department may adopt rules to cover the timely application for and issuance of registration receipts and insignia by mail or through an electronic off-premises location.

Transferred and redesignated from Transportation Code, Section 502.101 by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B. 2357 ), Sec. 114, eff. January 1, 2012.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 502. Registration of Vehicles
##### § 502.197 — Registration by Mail or Electronic Means; Service Charge

(a) A county assessor-collector may retain a service charge in the amount determined by the board under Section 502.1911 (Registration Processing and Handling Fee) from each applicant registering a vehicle by mail. The service charge shall be used to pay the costs of handling and postage to mail the registration receipt and insignia to the applicant.

(b) With the approval of the commissioners court of a county, a county assessor-collector may contract with a private entity to enable an applicant for registration to use an electronic off-premises location. A private entity may retain an amount determined by the board under Section 502.1911 (Registration Processing and Handling Fee) for the service provided.

(c) The department may adopt rules to cover the timely application for and issuance of registration receipts and insignia by mail or through an electronic off-premises location.

Transferred and redesignated from Transportation Code, Section 502.101 by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B. 2357 ), Sec. 114, eff. January 1, 2012.

*Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1287 (H.B.2202), Sec. 21.*

The service charge shall be used to pay the costs of handling and postage to mail the registration receipt and insignia to the applicant.

A private entity may retain an amount determined by the board under Section 502.1911 (Registration Processing and Handling Fee) for the service provided.
